operation.title: Import operation
operation.description: How to handle records in the target Airtable table before importing. 'append' keeps existing records and adds new ones. 'override' deletes all existing records first, then imports. 'create' will create the table if it does not exist, otherwise it behaves like 'append'.

clearOnCreate.title: Clear existing data when table exists (create mode only)
clearOnCreate.description: Only applies when operation is 'create'. If true and the table already exists, all existing records will be deleted before importing (similar to 'override' mode). If false and the table already exists, an error will be thrown. If the table does not exist, it will be created regardless of this setting.

base.title: Airtable base name or ID
base.description: The ID or name of the Airtable base where data will be imported (for example: appXXXXXXXXXXXXXX).

table.title: Airtable table name
table.description: Name of the Airtable table to import into. If operation is 'create' and this table does not exist, the Actor will try to create it (if Airtable permissions and APIs allow).

datasetId.title: Apify dataset ID
datasetId.description: ID of the Apify dataset that contains the records you want to import.

uniqueId.title: Unique ID source field (optional)
uniqueId.description: Source field (using dot notation, e.g. 'url' or 'metadata.headers.age') used to detect duplicates. If provided, the Actor reads the mapped target field in Airtable and skips records whose ID already exists there.

dataMappings.title: Field mappings
dataMappings.description: List of mappings from Apify dataset fields (source) to Airtable fields (target). 'source' uses dot notation (e.g. 'metadata.headers.age'), 'target' is the Airtable field name. 'targetType' is 'existing' or 'new'. 'fieldType' describes the Airtable field type (e.g. 'singleLineText', 'multilineText', 'number').

source.title: Source field
source.description: Field path from the Apify dataset item. Supports dot notation (e.g. 'metadata.description').

target.title: Target Airtable field
target.description: Airtable field name where the value should be stored.

targetType.title: Target type
targetType.description: 'existing' if the field already exists in Airtable, 'new' if it should be created when missing.

fieldType.title: Field type
fieldType.description: Airtable field type to use when creating a new field (e.g. 'singleLineText', 'multilineText', 'number', 'checkbox').
